data.title: Manhattan judge bars ex-Palantir employees from recruiting staff for Percepta AI
data.description:
A federal judge in Manhattan on Wednesday approved Palantir Technologies’ (PLTR) request to bar two former employees from recruiting its staff to join the company they started last year, Percepta AI. However, the judge declined to prohibit the ex-employees from continuing to work at their new venture.

The ruling by U.S. District Judge Paul Oetken will be in place pending the outcome of Palantir's lawsuit filed in October.

Palantir (PLTR) sued Percepta co-founder and CEO Hirsh Jain last year, claiming he “began an aggressive campaign to recruit numerous Palantir employees” after leaving the company in August 2024. 

Oetken also barred Joanna Cohen, a former Palantir engineer now with Percepta, from breaching a confidentiality agreement she had signed with Palantir, according to media reports.

But the judge denied Palantir's request to block the defendants from violating agreements they signed not to compete with the company or solicit its customers, which would have barred them from working at Percepta indefinitely.

Percepta is owned by venture capital firm General Catalyst and was publicly unveiled in October.

data.title: Materialise GAAP EPS of $0.12, revenue of $82.44M beats by $0.42M; initiates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Materialise press release (MTLS): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.12.
  • Revenue of $82.44M beats by $0.42M, increased by 6.8% Y/Y.
  • Adjusted EBITDA increased to 9,524 kEUR for the fourth quarter of 2025 from 4,306 kEUR for the 2024 period. Adjusted EBIT amounted to 3,980 kEUR, compared to (1,195) kEUR for the same period in 2024.
  • Cash flow from operating activities for the fourth quarter of the year 2025 was 5,274 kEUR, compared to 6,218 kEUR for the same period in 2024.  
  • Total capital expenditures for the fourth quarter of the year 2025 amounted to 4,411 kEUR.
  • The generated free cash flow over the fourth quarter of 2025 amounted to 4,511 kEUR.
  • Outlook: "On a consolidated level, we therefore expect our full year revenues for 2026 to grow to a range of 273,000 to 283,000 kEUR. We will continue investing in our Materialise Medical and Software segments while maintaining disciplined cost control and optimization, in particular in our Materialise Manufacturing segment and in our overhead. As a result, we expect our Adjusted EBIT to reach 10,000 to 12,000 kEUR for fiscal year 2026.”

data.title: Aegon reports FY results
data.description:
• Aegon press release (AEG): Full-year 2025 net result of EUR 980 million, up 45% compared with 2024.
  • Full-year 2025 operating result of EUR 1.7 billion, up 15% compared with 2024.
  • fFull-year 2025 OCG of EUR 1.3 billion, Aegon exceeds its target of EUR 1.2 billion.
  • Free cash flow of EUR 388 million, contributes to full-year free cash flow of EUR 829 million, consistent with the target of around EUR 800 million.
  • 2025 final dividend of EUR 0.21 per common share proposed, an increase of 11% compared with 2024 final dividend, enabling Aegon to meet its EUR 0.40 target for full-year 2025.

data.title: Airbus SE GAAP EPS of Є3.27 beats by Є0.50, revenue of Є25.98B misses by Є790M; gives FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Airbus SE press release (EADSF): Q4 GAAP EPS of Є3.27 beats by Є0.50.
  • Revenue of Є25.98B (+5.1% Y/Y) misses by Є790M.
  • Q4 2025 EBIT Adjusted increased by 17%.
  • Outlook:  The Company’s 2026 guidance is before M&A and includes the impact of currently applicable tariffs. On that basis, the Company targets to achieve in 2026:
  • Around 870 commercial aircraft deliveries;
  • EBIT Adjusted of around € 7.5 billion;
  • Free Cash Flow before Customer Financing of around € 4.5 billion.

data.title: Bill Gates withdraws from India AI summit
data.description:
• Microsoft (MSFT) co-founder Bill Gates withdrew from India’s AI Impact summit just hours before he was set to deliver the keynote address on Thursday.
  • “After careful consideration, and to ensure the focus remains on the AI Summit’s key priorities, Mr. Gates will not be delivering his keynote address,” the Gates Foundation said in a statement just hours before he was due to speak. No reason was provided for the withdrawal.

 

  • Nvidia (NVDA) announced on Saturday that its CEO, Jensen Huang, will not attend the India AI Impact Summit, a major gathering of tech leaders taking place in the country’s capital city of New Delhi.
  • Prime Minister Narendra Modi is scheduled to address the event on Thursday, alongside French President Emmanuel Macron, Google (GOOG) CEO Sundar Pichai, OpenAI (OPENAI) CEO Sam Altman and Anthropic (ANTHRO) CEO Dario Amodei.

data.title: Rio Tinto Non-GAAP EPS of $6.69 beats by $0.04, revenue of $57.64B beats by $1.33B
data.description:
• Rio Tinto press release (RIO): FY Non-GAAP EPS of $6.69 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $57.64B (+7.4% Y/Y) beats by $1.33B.
  • Underlying EBITDA to $25.4 billion and operating cash flow of $16.8 billion.
